title: engelhardia pterocarpa
intro: Engelhardia pterocarpa, also known as the Engelhardt's tree or Chinese parasol tree, is a deciduous tree species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in China, India, and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Engelhardia pterocarpa include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark, leaves, and roots are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are traditionally consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
